meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Stop Slow Development Speed
content.seo_description: Overcome slow development speed with expert project management. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your student portfolio projects today.
content.answer_block: Slow development speed stems from poor scoping and fragmented workflows. You solve this by implementing an AI-augmented project management framework. By integrating Antigravity for structured task orchestration and Claude Code for rapid iterative implementation, you move from conceptual paralysis to shipping production-ready features in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
You sit down at your terminal, coffee cooling, ready to build the next big thing. Three hours later, you’ve refactored the same component five times, your folder structure is a chaotic labyrinth, and you haven't shipped a single functional line of code. This is the 'Beginner’s Bottleneck'—the silent killer of student dreams and the primary reason most portfolio projects never see the light of day.

But the gap between 'idea' and 'MVP' isn't talent; it's architecture. By treating your code like an engineering project rather than a messy stream of consciousness, you reclaim your momentum. We are going to replace your scattered workflow with a high-velocity system using Antigravity and Claude Code, transforming you from a frustrated student into a methodical AI Builder.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Scope
steps.description: Break your project into micro-tasks that take no longer than 60 minutes to complete, preventing scope creep before it starts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and break them down into an atomic task list. For each task, provide a clear acceptance criteria and estimated complexity level.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Analyze codebase and generate a task-list.md file based on project goals'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the project structure, ensuring best practices are baked into your directory layout from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a clean architecture for a full-stack SaaS application using Next.js and Tailwind. Ensure modular folders for API, components, and hooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Create directory structure and initialize project files based on scaffold plan'

steps.title: Implement Iterative Logic
steps.description: Focus on building one feature at a time, using Claude Code to handle the boilerplate while you review the logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft the logic for user authentication using Supabase. Focus on secure session handling and clean error reporting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Implement user authentication module with Supabase'

steps.title: Automated Testing Cycles
steps.description: Never ship broken code. Use AI to generate test cases for every new feature you build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest unit tests for the authentication module created in the previous step, covering edge cases like invalid emails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Run unit tests and fix any failing assertions'

steps.title: Continuous Documentation
steps.description: Keep your project maintainable by having AI document your changes as you commit them.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a concise README update detailing the new authentication feature and how to configure environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Update README.md and commit changes with a descriptive message'

pro_tips: Never start coding until your task list is granular enough to feel trivial; if a task feels daunting, it isn't small enough.
Use Claude Code in 'read-only' mode on legacy codebases to understand existing architecture before making changes.
Treat your terminal as a command center; keep a dedicated window open for AI orchestration to maintain context flow.
Prioritize 'Happy Path' development first; don't get bogged down in edge-case error handling until the core feature works.
Standardize your commit messages using AI to ensure your GitHub history looks professional for recruiters.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is live, focus on deploying to Vercel and gathering feedback from one peer to iterate on your UI design.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to outpace 99% of your peers. Stop guessing and start shipping with precision. The transition from student to developer happens at the keyboard, one focused session at a time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
